subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 - Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 6 players.
- **Setup split found** — 2-4 players use only Tokyo City.
- **Setup split found** — 5-6 players use Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay.
- **Rules split found** — 5-6 entry rule for Tokyo Bay when City is occupied.
- **Rules split found** — At 4 or fewer remaining players, Tokyo Bay empties immediately (with City-empty exception).
- **Solo mode** — Not listed in provided rule text.
- **Scaling table** — No component-count table by player count is listed in provided excerpt.

#### Boards and figures
- **Tokyo board** — 1 board (Tokyo City + Tokyo Bay).
- **Monster boards** — 1 per monster (with Life and Victory dials).
- **Monster figures** — Matching cardboard figures.

#### Dice and tokens
- **Black dice** — 6 core dice.
- **Green dice** — 2 dice (used by Power-card effects).
- **Status tokens** — 28 total: 3 Smoke, 1 Mimic, 12 Shrink, 12 Poison.

#### Cards and resources
- **Power cards** — 1 deck (draw market of 3 face-up).
- **Energy cubes** — Shared pool used to buy/activate effects.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Core table setup
1. **Center board** — Place Tokyo board in the center.
2. **Card deck** — Shuffle Power cards into a face-down deck.
3. **Market row** — Reveal 3 face-up Power cards beside Tokyo.
4. **Token pool** — Place all 28 tokens near the board.
5. **Dice area** — Put 6 black dice at center.
6. **Green dice** — Set 2 green dice aside until card effects allow them.
7. **Energy pool** — Form one common pool of Energy cubes.

--- 2-4 Players ---
- **Tokyo zones in use** — Use exactly 1 zone: Tokyo City only.

--- 5-6 Players ---
- **Tokyo zones in use** — Use 2 zones: Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Monster choice** — Each player chooses 1 Monster.
- **Player kit** — Take matching Monster board and figure.
- **Starting Life** — Set Life to 10.
- **Starting VP** — Set Victory Points to 0.
- **Figure location** — Keep figure near your board until you enter Tokyo.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Determine first player
- **Roll-off** — Each player rolls the 6 black dice.
- **Start condition** — Most Claw results goes first.
- **Tie rule** — Tied players reroll until one has the highest Claw count.

#### Initial Tokyo state
- **Start occupancy** — No Monster starts in Tokyo.
- **First-turn obligation** — First player must enter Tokyo City during Enter Tokyo step if Tokyo is empty.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn order
- **Direction** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Turn sequence (in order)
1. **Roll Dice** — Roll up to 3 times total.
2. **Resolve Dice** — Resolve all final symbols.
3. **Enter Tokyo** — Enter if required by occupancy rules.
4. **Buy Power Cards** — Buy and/or sweep while you can pay.
5. **End of Turn** — Resolve end-of-turn effects; pass dice left.

#### Step 1: Roll Dice details
- **First roll** — Roll all 6 black dice.
- **Green dice condition** — Roll 1-2 green dice only if a card effect allows it.
- **Selective rerolls** — Before roll 2 and roll 3, keep any dice and reroll others.
- **Re-open kept dice** — On roll 3, you may reroll previously kept dice.

#### Step 2: Resolve Dice details
- **Resolution order** — Any order is allowed.
- **Mandatory resolution** — Every final die result must be resolved.

--- 2-4 Players ---
#### Step 3: Enter Tokyo
- **Empty Tokyo rule** — If no one is in Tokyo, active player must enter Tokyo City (the only Tokyo zone in 2-4 player games).

--- 5-6 Players ---
#### Step 3: Enter Tokyo
- **Empty Tokyo rule** — If both zones are empty, active player must enter Tokyo City.
- **Bay entry rule** — If Tokyo City is occupied and Tokyo Bay is empty, active player must enter Tokyo Bay (second zone used only in 5-6 player games).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Number dice (1/2/3) -> VP
- **Set scoring** — Three of a kind scores VP equal to that number.
- **Extra match bonus** — Each additional matching die adds +1 VP.
- **Example** — Four 2s score 3 VP total.

#### Energy dice -> Energy cubes
- **Gain rate** — Gain 1 Energy per Energy symbol rolled.
- **Storage** — Keep Energy in your reserve until spent.

#### Claw dice -> Damage
- **Damage rate** — Each Claw deals 1 Life loss to legal targets.
- **Inside-Tokyo targeting** — If you are in Tokyo, all Monsters outside Tokyo lose Life.
- **Outside-Tokyo targeting** — If you are outside Tokyo, all Monsters in Tokyo lose Life.
- **Tokyo mutual hit limit** — Monsters in Tokyo do not Claw each other via this targeting rule.

#### Heart dice -> Healing
- **Outside-Tokyo heal** — Heal 1 Life per Heart rolled.
- **Life cap** — You cannot heal above 10 Life.
- **Inside-Tokyo lock** — Heart dice do not heal while in Tokyo (unless card text says otherwise).

#### Elimination and discards
- **Elimination trigger** — A Monster at 0 Life is eliminated immediately.
- **Discard on elimination** — Eliminated Monster discards all Keep cards and all Energy cubes.

#### Yield timing
- **Yield condition** — A Monster in Tokyo may Yield only after taking Claw damage from another Monster.
- **Damage first** — Yield decision happens after that damage is applied.
- **Card-damage exception** — Damage from Power cards does not grant Yield by itself.

#### Buy Power Cards step options
- **Buy option** — Spend Energy equal to a face-up card's cost to buy it.
- **Immediate refill** — Replace purchased cards immediately from deck.
- **Sweep option** — Spend 2 Energy to discard all 3 face-up cards and reveal 3 new ones.
- **Repeat purchases** — Keep buying/sweeping in this step while you can pay.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Tokyo bonuses and constraints
- **Entry bonus** — Entering Tokyo gives +1 VP immediately.
- **Start bonus** — Starting your turn in Tokyo gives +2 VP.
- **Healing restriction** — Monsters in Tokyo cannot heal from Heart dice.

--- 2-4 Players ---
#### Tokyo structure
- **Active zone** — Use 1 active zone: Tokyo City only.
- **Yield flow** — Yield/replace interactions happen only around Tokyo City.

--- 5-6 Players ---
#### Tokyo structure
- **Active zones** — Use 2 active zones: Tokyo City and Tokyo Bay are both "in Tokyo."
- **Bay occupancy rule** — If City is occupied and Bay is empty, entrants must take Bay.
- **Player-count drop rule** — Once game has 4 or fewer players remaining, Tokyo Bay must be vacated immediately.
- **City-empty exception** — If Tokyo City is empty when Bay must vacate, move from Bay to City instead.

#### Power card types
- **Keep cards** — Stay face up until game end unless removed by effect.
- **Discard cards** — Resolve immediately when bought, then discard.

#### Timing glossary hooks
- **Roll definition** — You choose what to keep/reroll before each roll.
- **Yield definition** — Yield occurs only after Claw damage from another Monster, before the attacker's Enter Tokyo resolution.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Fire Breathing exception** — In a 2-player game, Fire Breathing causes the single opponent to lose only **1** Life Point total (not 2, since the opponent is not counted as both "left" and "right").

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End triggers
- **VP victory** — First Monster to reach 20 VP wins.
- **Survivor victory** — Last Monster remaining alive wins.

#### VP sources checklist
- **Number sets** — VP from 1/2/3 set scoring (plus extra-match bonuses).
- **Tokyo entry** — +1 VP when entering Tokyo.
- **Tokyo start** — +2 VP when starting your turn in Tokyo.
- **Card effects** — Any VP granted by Power-card text.

#### Elimination path
- **Combat finish** — You can win by eliminating all opponents before anyone reaches 20 VP.

#### Tie/simultaneous handling
- **Rulebook clarity** — Provided text gives win conditions but no formal tie-break paragraph.
- **Resolution method** — Resolve in normal turn/effect order; if still inseparable, table may treat as shared win.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early priorities
- **Energy floor** — Secure early Energy gains so card-buy turns start quickly.
- **Tokyo timing** — Enter Tokyo when your Life total can survive immediate counterattacks.
- **Roll discipline** — Lock dice toward one objective (VP set, Energy burst, or damage spike) instead of rerolling randomly.
- **Threat read** — Pivot from VP race to Claw pressure when opponents are near elimination.

--- 2-4 Players ---
- **Tokyo pressure** — With 1 Tokyo zone, retaliation is concentrated; plan exits early.

--- 5-6 Players ---
- **Dual-zone planning** — With 2 Tokyo zones (City + Bay), track both occupancies before committing to aggression.
- **Count-drop awareness** — Prepare for Bay collapse when table drops to 4 players.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Overstaying Tokyo** — Staying in Tokyo at low Life often converts a VP lead into elimination.
- **Energy neglect** — Skipping Energy early delays card power and flexibility.
- **Rigid number-chasing** — Forcing number sets every turn ignores urgent heal/damage opportunities.
- **Heart misread** — Forgetting Hearts do not heal in Tokyo causes avoidable knockouts.
- **Bad Yield timing** — Yielding too early gives tempo away; yielding too late risks elimination.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: VP race vs knockout line
- **VP line** — Prioritize number sets and Tokyo scoring.
- **Combat line** — Prioritize Claws to force eliminations.

#### Decision 2: Spend Energy now vs save
- **Immediate buy** — Stabilizes current turn power.
- **Bank + sweep** — Saving Energy enables market resets and multi-buy turns.

#### Decision 3: Hold Tokyo vs Yield
- **Hold** — Gains faster VP but blocks Heart healing and draws fire.
- **Yield** — Preserves Life and repositions, but may hand opponent scoring tempo.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for skimmable scan-first reading with explicit player-count audit and exact count headers for 2-4 and 5-6 player differences.
